meta.what: Northern light-minus-dark half-year: signed duration and the amplitude envelope, from Laskar La2004.
meta.source: Laskar et al. 2004, A&A 428, 261. Files INSOLN/INSOLP.LA2004.BTL.ASC (IMCCE, ssp.imcce.fr). Columns t[kyr] e obliquity[rad] varpi[rad], step 1 kyr.
meta.formula: signed = (4*T/pi)*e*sin(varpi); envelope = (4*T/pi)*e; T = 365.2422 d; varpi = heliocentric longitude of perihelion (col 4).
meta.time_origin: t is kyr from J2000; year = 2000 + 1000*t.
meta.caveat: Era LENGTHS and AMPLITUDES are robust across the whole span; calendar DATES of individual events are NOT what this data gives (that is Phase I's DE441 job, and only within ~15 kyr of the present).
